code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to clean object strings (remove surrounding quotes)
    def _verb_be(subject: str) -> str:
        """Return appropriate form of 'to be' for the subject (is/are)."""
        # Simple heuristic: use 'are' if subject ends with 's' (common for plural team names)
        stripped = subject.strip()
        return "are" if stripped.endswith("s") else "is"
    import re
    def _clean(val: str) -> str:
        """Strip whitespace, quotes, and parenthetical units."""
        v = val.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
        # Remove parenthetical content e.g. "96.1632 (kilograms)" -> "96.1632"
        v = re.sub(r"\s*\(.*?\)", "", v).strip()
        return v

    def _format_date(date_str: str) -> str:
        """Convert YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-M-D to a readable format like 'May 30, 1996'."""
        try:
            parts = [int(p) for p in date_str.split("-")]
            if len(parts) == 3:
                year, month, day = parts
                import calendar
                month_name = calendar.month_name[month]
                return f"{month_name} {day}, {year}"
        except Exception:
            pass
        return date_str

    # Mapping from predicate to a formatting lambda (subject, object) -> phrase
    predicate_map = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {_format_date(_clean(o))}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {_clean(o)}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {_clean(o)}",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {_clean(o)} meters tall",
        "weight": lambda s, o: f"{s} weighs {_clean(o)} kilograms",
        "position": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays as a {_clean(o)}",
        # Use possessive phrasing for club relationships
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s club is {_clean(o)}",
        "currentclub": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s current club is {_clean(o)}",
        "formerTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} formerly played for {_clean(o)}",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {_clean(o)}",
        # Revised phrasing for clearer ownership / roles
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is the manager of {s}",
        "coach": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is the coach of {s}",
        "draftTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s draft team is {_clean(o)}",
        "draftYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was drafted in {_clean(o)}",
        "draftRound": lambda s, o: f"{s} was in draft round {_clean(o)}",
        "draftPick": lambda s, o: f"{s} was the {_clean(o)}th pick",
        "playerNumber": lambda s, o: f"{s} wears number {_clean(o)}",
        "currentteam": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently plays for {_clean(o)}",
        "debutTeam": lambda s, o: f"{s} made his debut for {_clean(o)}",
        # Additional predicates beyond the athlete domain
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is the leader of {s}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {_clean(o)}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {_clean(o)}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {_clean(o)}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} {_verb_be(s)} based in the city of {_clean(o)}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} owns {s}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is the chairman of {s}",
        "generalManager": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is the general manager of {s}",
        "stadium": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s stadium is {_clean(o)}",
        # University team relationship
        "universityTeam": lambda s, o: f"The {s} are the university team for {_clean(o)}",
        # Prefer “play at” phrasing for grounds/stadiums
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s} play at {_clean(o)}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"The anthem of {s} is {_clean(o)}",
        # Phrase ethnic groups from the perspective of the group
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} are an ethnic group in {s}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{_clean(o)} is spoken in {s}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {_clean(o)}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {_clean(o)}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"The demonym for {s} is {_clean(o)}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {_clean(o)}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} covers an area of {_clean(o)}",
        "utcOffset": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a UTC offset of {_clean(o)}",
        "timeZone": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the {_clean(o)} time zone",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"The season for {s} is {_clean(o)}",
        "youthclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} was a member of the youth club {_clean(o)}",
    }

    # Helper to build a relative clause for an entity that appears as an object
    def _relative_clause(entity: str) -> str:
        if entity not in subject_map:
            return ""
        rel_parts = []
        for p, o in subject_map[entity]:
            # avoid recursive loops by not referencing the same entity again
            if o == entity:
                continue
            phrase = predicate_map.get(p, lambda s, o: f"{s} {p} {o}")(entity, o)
            rel_parts.append(phrase)
        return ", ".join(rel_parts)

    # Group triples by subject to build a compact description per entity
    subject_map = {}
    for t in triples:
        subject_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))

    def join_parts(parts):
        """Join a list of strings with commas and an 'and' before the last element."""
        if not parts:
            return ""
        if len(parts) == 1:
            return parts[0]
        return ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + " and " + parts[-1]

    sentences = []
    for subj, pred_objs in subject_map.items():
        phrase_parts = []
        club_objs = []
        # Process predicates
        for pred, obj in pred_objs:
            if pred in {"club", "currentclub"}:
                club_objs.append(obj)
            else:
                phrase = predicate_map.get(pred, lambda s, o: f"{s} {pred} {o}")(subj, obj)
                # If the object itself has descriptive triples (e.g., city), add a brief relative clause
                if obj in subject_map:
                    # handle common descriptive predicates like city, country, ground, stadium, leader
                    rel_parts = []
                    for p2, o2 in subject_map[obj]:
                        if p2 == "city":
                            rel_parts.append(f"based in {_clean(o2)}")
                        elif p2 == "country":
                            rel_parts.append(f"in {_clean(o2)}")
                        elif p2 == "ground":
                            rel_parts.append(f"with ground {_clean(o2)}")
                        elif p2 == "stadium":
                            rel_parts.append(f"whose stadium is {_clean(o2)}")
                        elif p2 == "leader":
                            rel_parts.append(f"led by {_clean(o2)}")
                    if rel_parts:
                        phrase += ", which " + " and ".join(rel_parts)
                phrase_parts.append(phrase)

        # Build club-related phrase with possible coach information
        if club_objs:
            # Build the main club phrase (singular or plural)
            if len(club_objs) == 1:
                club_phrase = f"{subj} plays for {club_objs[0]}"
            else:
                clubs_str = " and ".join(club_objs)
                club_phrase = f"{subj} plays for {clubs_str}"
            # Add relative clauses (manager, coach, owner, chairman, generalManager) for the first club
            first_club = club_objs[0]
            if first_club in subject_map:
                rel_clauses = []
                for p, o in subject_map[first_club]:
                    if p == "manager":
                        rel_clauses.append(f"managed by {_clean(o)}")
                    elif p == "coach":
                        rel_clauses.append(f"coached by {_clean(o)}")
                    elif p == "owner":
                        rel_clauses.append(f"owned by {_clean(o)}")
                    elif p == "chairman":
                        rel_clauses.append(f"chaired by {_clean(o)}")
                    elif p == "generalManager":
                        rel_clauses.append(f"with general manager {_clean(o)}")
                if rel_clauses:
                    club_phrase += f", which is " + " and ".join(rel_clauses)
            # Ensure club phrase appears early
            phrase_parts.insert(0, club_phrase)

        # Combine all parts for this subject
        subject_clause = join_parts(phrase_parts)
        sentences.append(subject_clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    final_text = join_parts(sentences) + "."
    return final_text

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

                            rel_parts.append(f"led by {_clean(o2)}")

                phrase_parts.append(phrase)

        if club_objs:

            else:

                club_phrase = f"{subj} plays for {clubs_str}"

            first_club = club_objs[0]

                for p, o in subject_map[first_club]:

                    elif p == "chairman":
                        rel_clauses.append(f"chaired by {_clean(o)}")
                    elif p == "generalManager":
                        rel_clauses.append(f"with general manager {_clean(o)}")
                if rel_clauses:
                    club_phrase += f", which is " + " and ".join(rel_clauses)
            # Ensure club phrase appears early
            phrase_parts.insert(0, club_phrase)

        # Combine all parts for this subject
        subject_clause = join_parts(phrase_parts)
        sentences.append(subject_clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if not sentences:
        return ""
    final_text = join_parts(sentences) + "."
    return final_text

# Task
Suggest an improvement to the current program that will improve the combined_score.
Different solutions with similar combined_score score but different ideas are valuable.
